question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user observe that isEnabled() returns false (e.g., via a public health endpoint) and exploit the resulting parsing gap to cause the mirror node to miss an ordered sequence of stream files, leading to a permanent hole in the stored transaction history that makes prior transactions appear to have never occurred?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user trigger a condition (e.g., repeated malformed requests that cause an unhandled exception in the parser pipeline) that causes setEnabled(false) to be invoked programmatically, halting all stream file parsing and allowing a window of transactions to be permanently excluded from the mirror node history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the default getFrequency() value of 20ms (for RECORD type) by flooding the stream file queue faster than the parser can consume, causing the BatchStreamFileNotifier queue to overflow and silently drop stream files, resulting in gaps in the persisted transaction sequence?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario where getProcessingTimeout() (default 10s) expires before a large batch of transactions is committed, causing the health indicator to report DOWN and triggering an operator-initiated parser reset that skips already-queued stream files and reorders the visible transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.queueCapacity (default 10 for RECORD) by submitting transactions that cause the importer to generate exactly queueCapacity+1 stream files in rapid succession, causing the blocking put() in BatchStreamFileNotifier to stall and reorder the sequence in which files are flushed to the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.maxItems (default 60,000 for RECORD) threshold by submitting exactly maxItems transactions in a single file, causing the batch to flush prematurely and split what should be an atomic multi-file batch into two separate database transactions, creating an inconsistent intermediate state in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.flushInterval (default 2s) by timing transaction submissions so that two logically related stream files land in different flush windows, causing them to be committed in separate database transactions and making the history appear non-contiguous to downstream REST API consumers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.window (default 5m) catch-up batching logic by submitting a burst of transactions that pushes the importer more than 5 minutes behind, causing the batch window to activate and merge files from different time periods into a single batch, potentially reordering the consensus timestamp sequence stored in the database?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.maxFiles (default 1 for RECORD) limit by causing the importer to receive multiple stream files simultaneously, forcing the parser to process them one at a time and creating a race condition between the single-file and multi-file parse() overloads that results in duplicate or out-of-order entries in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getStreamType() for BALANCE returns a non-chained type (isChained() == false) to inject a crafted balance file with a manipulated consensus timestamp that, when parsed, overwrites a legitimate transaction's balance snapshot and makes the historical balance state appear to have been different at a prior point in time?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user submit transactions that cause the RECORD StreamType parser and the BALANCE StreamType parser to process overlapping consensus time windows simultaneously (since they run on separate threads in BatchStreamFileNotifier), leading to a race condition where balance snapshots are stored with timestamps that contradict the transaction ordering in the record stream?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamFileHealthIndicator's dependency on isEnabled() by causing repeated parse failures that flip the health status to DOWN, which in a self-healing deployment triggers an automatic pod restart that clears the in-memory last AtomicReference, causing the parser to re-read findLatest() from the database and potentially re-process or skip files depending on what was committed before the restart?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a transaction that causes the record file parser's @Transactional method to exceed the transactionTimeout (default 120s), triggering a transaction rollback that leaves the in-memory last AtomicReference pointing to a stream file that was never committed to the database, causing the next parse() call to skip the hash chain check against the actual last committed file and accept a file with a mismatched previousHash?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BatchProperties.queueCapacity=1 for BALANCE type by flooding the system with balance-affecting transactions, causing the balance file queue to block indefinitely on put(), starving the record stream parser of CPU and causing record stream files to be processed out of their natural time order relative to balance files?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the difference in getFrequency() between BALANCE (1s) and RECORD (20ms) parsers to create a timing window where a record file referencing a balance state is parsed before the corresponding balance file, causing the mirror node to store a transaction history that references a balance state that does not yet exist in the balance table?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: setEnabled(boolean)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a publicly accessible actuator endpoint (e.g., Spring Boot /actuator/env or /actuator/configprops) that is misconfigured to allow writes, to call setEnabled(false) on the RecordParserProperties bean, halting record file parsing and causing all subsequent transactions to be absent from the mirror node history until the service is restarted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between BatchProperties.flushInterval and BatchProperties.window to cause the batch subscriber thread to flush an incomplete batch (fewer files than maxFiles) at the flushInterval boundary, then immediately start a new batch with the remaining files, creating two separate database commits for what should have been a single atomic batch and introducing a visible gap in the transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getProcessingTimeout()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a large volume of transactions that causes the importer to fall behind by more than getProcessingTimeout() (10s), triggering the health indicator to report DOWN, which in a Kubernetes liveness probe setup causes the pod to be killed and restarted, resetting the parser state and causing it to re-download and re-parse files from the last committed point, potentially creating duplicate transaction entries if the commit was partial?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the ArrayBlockingQueue used for BatchProperties.queueCapacity by causing the producer (downloader) to block on queue.put() while the consumer (parser) is in the middle of a database transaction, creating a deadlock-like condition where neither the current batch commits nor new files are accepted, effectively freezing the transaction history at a specific point in time?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that isEnabled() is checked inside shouldParse() (in AbstractStreamFileParser) but the in-memory last AtomicReference is only updated on successful parse, so if parsing is disabled mid-stream and then re-enabled, the parser resumes from the last in-memory reference rather than the database, potentially skipping files that arrived during the disabled window?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxItems=60,000 threshold in BatchProperties by crafting transactions that each generate exactly 1 item, submitting 59,999 transactions in one file and 1 in the next, causing the batch to flush after the second file rather than waiting for the window, and splitting a logically related set of transactions across two database commits?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the BLOCK StreamType (fileCloseInterval=500ms) versus RECORD StreamType (fileCloseInterval=2s) difference to submit transactions that arrive in a block file before the corresponding record file is processed, causing the BlockFileTransformer to produce a RecordFile with a previousHash that does not match the hash chain of the record stream, triggering a HashMismatchException that skips the file and creates a gap in history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the interaction between BatchProperties.maxFiles and the batch subscriber's buffer (ArrayList, unsynchronized) by causing exactly maxFiles files to be queued simultaneously, then submitting one more file that triggers a concurrent flush, leading to a race condition where the unsynchronized buffer is read and written by two threads simultaneously, corrupting the order of files passed to parse(List<T>)?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the 20ms polling frequency of the RECORD parser by submitting transactions at a rate that generates stream files faster than 20ms apart, causing the queue.poll(frequency, MILLISECONDS) call to return null before a file is available, triggering a handle(null) call that may flush an incomplete batch and commit a partial transaction history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/parser/ParserProperties.java -> Scope: High: Reorganizing transaction history without direct theft of funds'] [Function: getBatch()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the RetryProperties.maxAttempts=Integer.MAX_VALUE for RECORD type by submitting a transaction that causes a repeatable parse failure (e.g., a malformed but syntactically valid transaction), causing the parser to retry indefinitely and block all subsequent stream files from being processed, effectively freezing the transaction history at the point of the failing file?
